Job Title: Technical Solutions Engineer - Contract (OCR + LLM | .NET / Python | OpenAI)
Ad Text:
Senior Technical Solutions Engineer (Contract)
OCR + LLM | AI Product Enhancement

We’re working with a client delivering a real-world AI solution focused on product label extraction, and they’re looking for a Senior Technical Solutions Engineer to enhance an existing OCR + LLM pipeline.

Why apply:

Work on a live OCR + LLM solution — real-world AI, not theory
FULY REMOTE (within Australia)
Flexible environment, high-impact work, immediate start
What you’ll be doing:
Enhancing an existing OCR + LLM solution for product label extraction
Working across backend systems to improve data processing and accuracy
Integrating and refining OpenAI / LLM workflows
Improving performance, reliability, and scalability
Deploying and managing components in Docker environments
What you’ll bring:
Strong backend engineering experience (.NET and/or Python)
Experience working with OpenAI / LLM APIs (ideally Azure OpenAI)
Exposure to OCR or document processing pipelines
Experience with Docker / containerisation
Ability to hit the ground running in a fast-paced contract environment

Apply now via SEEK or email daniel@etegroup.com.au

ETE Group are specialist recruiters across Microsoft technologies, AI/LLM, and modern engineering. If you're hiring in this space or finding it tough to secure the right AI/LLM talent, feel free to reach out — always happy to help.

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.


About the role:

We’re seeking a Support System Architecture Team Lead to join the AIR6500 Operational Continuity team. In this role, you’ll lead a team of engineers while shaping how support systems are designed and delivered for one of Australia’s most advanced Defence programs.

This is a unique opportunity to define and lead the Support System architecture for AIR6500 by combining systems engineering, operational continuity, and sustainment to ensure the system is reliable, supportable and mission-ready from day one.

You’ll play a key role in designing how the system is supported across its lifecycle, working across engineering, logistics, ICT/cyber and sustainment teams to deliver a cohesive, fit-for-purpose Support System.

Key responsibilities

Lead the design and development of the AIR6500 Support System architecture ensuring alignment with operational continuity and sustainment needs

Manage and mentor a team of engineers, setting clear technical direction and priorities

Apply systems engineering principles across the full lifecycle

Collaborate across multidisciplinary teams including engineering, ILS, ICT, cyber, infrastructure and sustainment

Ownership to ensure the design meets supportability, reliability (RAM), safety, security and environmental requirements

Support design reviews, resolve technical challenges and maintain end-to-end design integrity

 

About you:

You’re an experienced Systems Engineer or Architect with a background in Operational Continuity, Support Systems or ILS and you enjoy leading teams while solving complex system-level challenges.

You bring a strong mix of technical depth and leadership, and are comfortable working across multiple disciplines to shape practical, long-term support solutions.

Essential skills and experience:

Strong experience in systems architecture and systems engineering lifecycle

Background in Operational Continuity, Support Systems or Integrated Logistics Support (ILS)

Experience leading or mentoring engineers and coordinating technical work

Ability to work across multi-disciplinary teams and complex stakeholder environments

Experience ensuring design integrity, integration and technical assurance across complex systems

Engineering degree (Systems, Electrical, Mechanical, Aerospace or similar)

An active NV-1 security clearance and ability to handle Export Controlled information

 

Why join us?

Lead and shape the Support System for a next-generation Defence capability

Combine systems engineering, sustainment and operational continuity in one role

Lead a high-performing team in a complex, multi-disciplinary environment

Work alongside experts across engineering, Defence and industry




How we reward and support you: 

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of work patterns

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs


Special Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. Inghams is seeking a talented AI Engineer to join our AI, Data & Digital Innovation team and drive production AI/ML capabilities that will transform how we operate at scale.  This is your opportunity to build advanced forecasting models, implement generative AI solutions, and establish agentic AI Systems at Australia and New Zealand's largest integrated poultry producer—a company that has been bringing families to the table for over 100 years.

We're in the midst of an exciting AI transformation journey, rolling out our cloud AI platform powered by AWS Bedrock.  As our AI Engineer, you'll be instrumental in our AI transformation—building production-ready AI systems at pace, deploying demand forecasting models that optimise inventory and production planning, and implementing agent architectures that enable autonomous AI workflows across our enterprise systems

Key responsibilities:

Rapidly design, build, and deploy production AI systems including agentic workflows, multi-agent orchestration, and autonomous decision-making, moving at pace from concept to production, leveraging foundation models and cloud AI services to deliver business value in weeks rather than months.
Navigate uncharted territory in AI implementation by experimenting with emerging techniques, novel architectures, and cutting-edge foundation model capabilities.
Build and iterate on generative AI solutions across diverse use cases including intelligent automation, conversational interfaces, and AI-powered insights.  Deploy production GenAI applications rapidly using platforms like AWS Bedrock and Anthropic Claude, optimising for quick wins and measurable business impact.
Implement sophisticated agent systems and tool-use patterns that autonomously interact with enterprise systems, pushing the boundaries of what AI can accomplish in operational environments.
Combine traditional ML techniques with modern generative AI capabilities to solve business challenges across multiple domains (forecasting, optimisation, insights, automation).
Implement agent core architecture on AWS, enabling autonomous AI workflows that integrate with our cloud platform and legacy ERP, manufacturing execution and operational systems
Utilise Claude AI and Claude Code to accelerate model development, experimentation and deployment cycles, driving rapid innovation and time-to-value
Collaborate with Data Engineering teams to establish robust MLOps pipelines, ensuring model monitoring, retraining and governance across production environments
Support consumer analytics and customer intelligence capabilities with advanced ML capabilities including forecasting, segmentation and predictive analytics
Work with business stakeholders and AI practitioners across the organisation to identify use cases, translate requirements into technical solutions and deliver measurable business outcomes—bridging the gap between AI capabilities and operational needs.
Develop and maintain model performance metrics, A/B testing frameworks and evaluation processes to ensure model accuracy, reliability and continuous improvement
Implement responsible AI practices, including bias detection, explainability and compliance with enterprise governance frameworks and AWS security standards
Coordinate with AWS engineers on advanced ML architecture patterns, leveraging their expertise to implement cutting-edge solutions and stay at the forefront of AWS AI/ML service capabilities.
Contribute to the AI transformation roadmap, exploring emerging technologies and architectural patterns that shape the strategic direction of our AI/ML capabilities through 2030.

Key requirements:

Strong machine learning fundamentals with hands-on experience developing, training, and deploying production ML models. 
AWS ML/AI expertise including SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, and related services, able to architect and implement end-to-end ML solutions on AWS, from data preprocessing to model deployment and monitoring.
Production Python experience with strong proficiency in ML libraries and experience writing production-quality code with proper testing, logging, and error handling.
MLOps and model lifecycle management experience, understanding how to version models, implement CI/CD for ML pipelines, monitor model performance in production, and build automated retraining workflows.
Generative AI and LLM experience including prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning, and agent architecture, with practical experience deploying GenAI solutions using platforms like AWS Bedrock, OpenAI or Anthropic Claude.
Model evaluation and experimentation, able to design A/B tests, implement proper train/validation/test splits, use cross-validation effectively, and select appropriate metrics for different business problems.
Business translation and communication, able to confidently explain ML concepts to non-technical stakeholders, translate business problems and communicate model limitations and trade-offs clearly.
4–6 years of hands-on ML engineering experience in commercial environments, with at least 2 years deploying and maintaining production ML systems at scale.
Proficiency with AI-powered development tools like Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, or similar platforms
Experience in FMCG, manufacturing, or supply chain environments is advantageous

Inghams is seeking a talented Senior Data Engineer to join our AI, Data & Digital Innovation team and play a foundational role in building enterprise-scale data capabilities that will power our business for the next decade.  This is your opportunity to architect and deliver production-grade data infrastructure at Australia and New Zealand's largest integrated poultry producer.

We're in the midst of an exciting digital transformation journey, rolling out our cloud data platform powered by AWS.  As our Senior Data Engineer, you'll be instrumental in delivering our data transformation initiative—integrating legacy ERP, manufacturing execution, and operational systems with modern cloud architecture, establishing the data foundation that enables AI/ML innovation, consumer insights, and intelligent automation across our operations.

Key responsibilities:

Deliver enterprise-scale data infrastructure at pace using AI-powered development tools to accelerate pipeline development, testing, and deployment cycles, and build production-ready data solutions rapidly through AI-assisted coding, automated testing frameworks, and modern DevOps practices that compress traditional delivery timelines.
Experiment with emerging AWS services, pioneering new architectural patterns, and implementing cutting-edge data engineering techniques.
Build scalable, AI-enabled data pipelines that serve as the foundation for ML/AI innovation, advanced analytics, and intelligent automation. Design data infrastructure that enables rapid experimentation, supports diverse AI/ML workloads, and unlocks new business capabilities across the enterprise.
Rapidly prototype and productionise data solutions that directly impact business outcomes, iterating quickly based on stakeholder feedback and operational metrics.
Champion a culture of rapid innovation and continuous experimentation, leveraging AI-assisted development to compress build cycles, improve code quality, and capture knowledge automatically.
Develop and optimise ETL/ELT processes to integrate legacy ERP, manufacturing execution, and operational systems with modern cloud-based data architecture
Implement data modelling and schema design for AWS services including S3 (data lake), Redshift (data warehouse), and supporting services, applying dimensional modelling and data vault techniques to ensure scalability and performance.
Establish data governance, quality frameworks and data contracts across enterprise data sources
Collaborate with AI, Analytics and BI teams to ensure data availability, discoverability, and fitness for purpose across use cases from operational reporting to advanced ML model training and real-time inference.
Support consumer analytics and customer intelligence capabilities with reliable data infrastructure, APIs, and integration patterns
Work with cross-functional teams to define data requirements, establish data contracts, and deliver against business-driven use cases
Maintain data security, compliance and access controls in line with enterprise governance frameworks and AWS best practices
Monitor, troubleshoot and improve data pipeline performance, reliability and cost-efficiency across the AWS environment, implementing observability, alerting, and automated remediation where appropriate.
Document data architecture, data flows and technical specifications to support knowledge sharing, platform scalability, and onboarding of future team members

Key requirements:

Strong AWS data engineering experience with hands-on expertise in S3, Redshift, Glue, Lambda, Step Functions, Athena, and related services
Production-grade Python and SQL skills with extensive experience building ETL/ELT pipelines, writing complex queries, optimising database performance, and implementing data quality frameworks.
Data modelling and architecture expertise with practical knowledge of dimensional modelling, data vault, schema design patterns, and understanding of how to structure data for both analytical and operational use cases.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC) proficiency using Terraform, CloudFormation, or CDK to provision and manage cloud data infrastructure in a repeatable, version-controlled manner.
CI/CD and DevOps practices with experience implementing automated testing, deployment pipelines, and monitoring for data infrastructure using tools like GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or AWS CodePipeline.
Data governance and quality mindset, understanding the importance of data contracts, lineage, validation, and metadata management
Business translation and communication ability to confidently explain technical data concepts to non-technical stakeholders, gather requirements from business users, and translate ambiguous needs into concrete technical solutions.
Problem-solving and systems thinking with a genuine interest in understanding business challenges, evaluating trade-offs, and delivering practical, maintainable solutions
6–8 years of hands-on data engineering experience in a commercial environment, with at least 3 years focused on cloud data platforms and production pipeline development.
Experience in FMCG, manufacturing, or supply chain environments is advantageous

Job Description

The team

At SEEK, our AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads that work within an APAC matrix organisational structure. The AI Platform Engineering teams have a specific mission to provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and continuously deliver intelligent, differentiated experiences at scale.

You will work with roles across the engineering spectrum from graduate to lead level, including Software Engineers, Engineering Managers, Heads of Engineering, Software Architects, Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Platform Engineers, Product Managers, Business Analysts, and Iteration Managers.

About the role 

A Staff Platform Engineer is a technical lead and senior member within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams. You'll be responsible for the technical strategy and direction of SEEK's GenAI and ML Platforms, ensuring these systems are highly available, resilient, and enable AI innovation at scale. Embedded within a team, you'll lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS and SEEK more broadly. Your primary focus is designing and building AI platform solutions that enable Data Scientists, Engineers, and product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences rapidly and safely. You bring in-depth technical understanding of AI platform infrastructure, multi-cloud architectures (AWS, GCP), and the operational requirements of GenAI and ML systems, while driving alignment of your team's technical roadmap to SEEK's overall AI strategy.

Responsibilities 

Design and evolve technical architecture for GenAI and ML platform systems, ensuring they are well-architected, highly available, and aligned with SEEK's development practices and technical metrics
Drive technical strategy that enables SEEK's AI innovation at scale, leading architecture decisions for multi-cloud AI infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and aligning platform capabilities with business objectives
Build and operate highly scalable, performant AI infrastructure using AWS and Python for platform automation, tooling, and integrations while exemplifying clean, well-tested, secure code
Lead cross-team technical initiatives that influence AI platform strategy across AIPS, serving as the key point of contact for technical solutions and system interfaces
Partner with Data Scientists and ML Engineers to enable self-service model development, deployment, and operation while strengthening engineering discipline through understanding of AI/ML operational requirements
Support the Engineering Manager in capacity planning, risk management, and scoping of technical work while building strong relationships with Architecture, Security, and other AI platform teams
Grow team member technical capabilities through coaching on AI/ML platform patterns and practices while fostering a culture of safety, inclusivity, and diversity
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Proven experience as a Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or similar technical leadership role building and operating production platform systems at scale
Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure and platform engineering, with deep hands-on experience in AWS services and multi-cloud architectures
Advanced Python programming skills with demonstrated experience building platform automation, tooling, and integrations for highly scalable systems
Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), container orchestration (Kubernetes), and observability tools for platform operations
Track record of leading technical roadmaps and driving platform health initiatives aligned with key technical metrics and SRE principles
Experience building platforms that enable self-service capabilities for engineering and data teams, with understanding of developer experience and platform adoption
Hands-on MLOps or ML Engineering experience, GenAI/LLM platform knowledge (model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns), GCP/Vertex AI experience, or ML lifecycle tooling expertise are good to have, but not a must.

Design, develop, and maintain software solutions to automate FTTP network workflows
Integrate network elements and platforms into OSS using APIs, scripts, and data interfaces
Develop and maintain scripts for network configuration, validation, deployment, and auditing

Support integration with both modern and legacy network equipment across various vendor interfaces
Troubleshoot automation, integration, and data issues across OSS and network systems
Collaborate with engineers, operations teams, and developers to meet operational requirements
Contribute to documentation, coding standards, and best practices in network automation
Continuously identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reliability, and scalability of automation processes

About you:

The ideal person for this role will have/be:

Passionate about software development and network automation
Focused and detail-oriented, especially with configuration, data, and automation logic
Curious and adaptable, eager to learn new technologies, tools, and network platforms
Comfortable working in complex environments with both modern and legacy systems
Highly collaborative, working effectively with cross-functional technical teams
Self-motivated, able to manage priorities and work independently
Clear and confident communicator, able to explain technical concepts to all stakeholders

Essential Experience:

Degree in Computer Systems Design, Computer Science, or a related field
Proven experience as a Software Developer, Automation Engineer, or similar role in a networked/infrastructure environment
Strong scripting and development skills (e.g., Python, Bash, or similar)
Experience integrating systems using APIs, data formats (JSON, XML), and automation frameworks
Skilled in extracting, transforming, and managing data from multiple systems
Solid understanding of networking concepts, ideally including FTTP, access networks, or service provider environments
Experience testing and validating scripts and automation workflows
Experience working with network devices is desirable
Familiarity with Postman or other API testing tools is highly desirable

This is not a standard support engineering role.
  
You’ll be working on complex, custom-built mechatronic and motion systems deployed globally in high-profile, technically demanding environments. These are precision machines combining mechanical engineering, automation, controls and motion technology. When something needs diagnosing, validating or improving, you’re the person stepping in.
  
The role sits within a high-performing Service and Support team and blends mechanical depth with applied PLC and motion troubleshooting. It is hands-on, analytical and collaborative.
  
The Role
You’ll sit within a high-performing Service & Support team, providing mechanical and controls engineering expertise across new and existing installations.
  
Day to day, you will:

• Design and modify mechanical and mechatronic components
• Maintain SolidWorks models and technical drawings
• Perform core engineering calculations and validation
• Troubleshoot PLC systems including Beckhoff and Allen Bradley
• Diagnose servo, hydraulic and electric motion issues
• Conduct root cause analysis on complex faults
• Produce clear technical documentation and service notes
• Support global clients remotely and attend site when required
• Collaborate closely with engineers, designers and technicians
  
What You’ll Bring

• Degree in Mechatronics, Mechanical Engineering or similar
• Strong mechanical fundamentals with hands-on troubleshooting experience
• SolidWorks capability
• PLC exposure ideally Beckhoff and/or Allen Bradley
• Exposure to custom machinery or safety-critical systems is advantageous
• Australian Citizen or PR
  
This role is not one you want to miss, so if you want to hear more please don't hesitate to reach out!
nat@justdigitalpeople.com.au

The Strategy and Performance Division promotes the value of education in South Australia and plays a key role in improving the public education system. It leads system-wide strategy, manages strategic risks, provides data-driven insights, and supports schools and preschools to succeed and strengthen their standing within their communities.

The Data Operations and Service directorate is responsible for ingesting, storing, organising, and managing data held in the Department’s data warehouses/repositories. It is the engine room of Strategy and Performance, ensuring that data is clean, high quality, usable and available. The team is also responsible for liaising with customers to assess and prioritise their requirements, and leading data governance operations for the Department. Data Operations and Service manages the full range of data life cycle functions, including business analysis, data engineering, product development, and testing.
